title: The complete academic record from Wuhan to UT Austin
summary: A comprehensive view of Gengchen Mai's educational and professional journey, documented through institutional profiles and official records.
curatorNote: This exhibit traces the progression from undergraduate GIS education in China through graduate training at UCSB, postdoctoral research at Stanford, faculty positions at UGA and UT Austin, and concurrent industry collaboration with Google Research.

comparativeBeats.title: Educational Foundation
comparativeBeats.text: B.S. in Geographic Information System from Wuhan University established the foundation in geospatial science, followed by M.A. and Ph.D. in Geography from UC Santa Barbara with emphasis in Cognitive Science.

comparativeBeats.title: Research Training
comparativeBeats.text: Doctoral research at UCSB's STKO Lab and Spatial Center focused on Geographic Question Answering and Spatially-Explicit Machine Learning, with industry internships at Esri, Apple Maps, and Google X.

comparativeBeats.title: Postdoctoral Transition
comparativeBeats.text: Stanford AI Lab postdoc positioned Mai at the intersection of artificial intelligence and spatial data science, bridging geography and computer science.

comparativeBeats.title: Faculty Career
comparativeBeats.text: First faculty position at UGA (2021-2024) established research program in GeoAI, earning AAG Nystrom Award. Current position at UT Austin (2024-present) directs SEAI Lab with NSF funding.

takeaways: Clear progression from GIS fundamentals to cutting-edge GeoAI research
Strong institutional backing at each career stage
Recognition through prestigious awards (AAG Nystrom, Garrison)
Active industry collaboration alongside academic positions

title: Recognition and funding validate GeoAI contributions
summary: Awards, grants, and institutional appointments demonstrate the impact and significance of Mai's research in spatially explicit AI and geographic data science.
curatorNote: This exhibit documents the external validation of Mai's research through competitive awards, federal funding, and institutional recognition.

comparativeBeats.title: Professional Awards
comparativeBeats.text: AAG Nystrom Award (2023) for research on polygonal geometry representation learning and AAG William L. Garrison Award (2022) recognize contributions to geographic information science.

comparativeBeats.title: Federal Research Funding
comparativeBeats.text: $300,000 NSF grant (2025) supports research on measuring and mitigating geographic bias in AI models, addressing critical challenges in trustworthy AI.

comparativeBeats.title: Industry Collaboration
comparativeBeats.text: Visiting Faculty Researcher position at Google Research demonstrates industry recognition and facilitates translation of academic research to practical applications.

comparativeBeats.title: Lab Leadership
comparativeBeats.text: Founded and directs SEAI Lab at UT Austin, establishing a research group focused on GeoAI, geo-foundation models, and geographic knowledge graphs.

takeaways: Competitive awards from leading professional organizations
Significant federal research funding for innovative work
Active collaboration with major tech companies
Leadership in establishing new research directions
